name: 20% heat-inactivated human serum with menadione and hemin (PMC10949514)
description: 20% heat-inactivated human serum with menadione and hemin — medium reported in PMC10949514 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC10949514 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium '20% heat-inactivated human serum (complex biological fluid; not a chemically defined mineral medium)' (No cited base-medium recipe. This is the assay medium defined in-paper: Nagai et al. (2024), 'Porphyromonas gingivalis PGN_1547 ...', PMC10949514. Organism grown anaerobically; serum is undiluted-basis 20% (v/v), the balance being the assay buffer/broth which the paper does not specify.). Complex medium: chemically-defined portion
provenance.verification: recuration attempted — recipe not confidently re-mappable (see notes)
provenance.verification_evidence: Growth curves of P. gingivalis ATCC 33277 and Δpgn_1547 mutant growing in 20% heat-inactivated human serum supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin ... P. gingivalis ATCC 33277 was cultured anaerobically at 37°C.
provenance.recipe_evidence: Figure 3a caption / Methods: 'Growth curves of P. gingivalis ATCC 33277 and Δpgn_1547 mutant growing in 20% heat-inactivated human serum supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin.' Cell maintenance and liquid cultures: 'Brain heart infusion (BHI)-blood agar plates ... supplemented with 5 µg/mL hemin and 1 µg/mL menadione'; 'Liquid cultures were made in tryptic soy broth (TSB) ... supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin.' Organism 'cultured anaerobically at 37°C.'
provenance.base_medium: 20% heat-inactivated human serum (complex biological fluid; not a chemically defined mineral medium)
provenance.base_medium_reference: No cited base-medium recipe. This is the assay medium defined in-paper: Nagai et al. (2024), 'Porphyromonas gingivalis PGN_1547 ...', PMC10949514. Organism grown anaerobically; serum is undiluted-basis 20% (v/v), the balance being the assay buffer/broth which the paper does not specify.

sources_chased.what: Full-text methods (Bacterial growth conditions) and Fig. 3a caption for the serum growth-curve medium
sources_chased.how: corpus_path JATS XML full text (Read + grep)
added: explicit menadione 1 µg/mL and hemin 5 µg/mL concentrations
serum listed as explicit 20% (v/v) complex component

sources_chased.what: Any cited base-medium reference / trace-element mix / vitamin stock for the serum medium
sources_chased.how: grep for cited refs, serum, diluent, PBS, trace, vitamin, SL-10, Widdel; none present
added: none — the serum medium has no cited base recipe; it is serum + menadione + hemin only, and human serum is decomposed separately by the DB as a complex ingredient

recuration.notes: This is a complex biological assay medium, not a chemically defined mineral medium, so there is no hidden trace-element/vitamin stock to expand. The paper fully defines the additives (menadione 1 µg/mL, hemin 5 µg/mL) in both the Fig. 3a caption and the Bacterial growth conditions section; the ~80% balance/diluent of the 20% serum is not stated by the authors and was NOT fabricated. Serum is retained as a named complex component for separate DB decomposition. conc_mM computed from menadione MW 172.18 and hemin MW 651.94 (1 and 5 mg/L respectively). Note: routine TSB/BHI-blood plates (also hemi
